---
title: "Lauren Price, M.P.P., R.T.S.B.A."
subtitle: "Houston, TX | 772-485-2635 | lauren.3.price@gmail.com | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lauren-price-data/) | [GitHub](https://github.com/pricele2) | [Calendly](https://calendly.com/lauren-3-price/quick-chat)"
format: PrettyPDF-pdf
---
## PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

Data and policy leader with 10+ years of experience in analytics, causal inference, forecasting, grants management, data governance, and financial operations in K-12 education, informing enrollment strategy, staffing, capital planning, program evaluation, compliance, and statewide policy.

## EDUCATION

**University of Washington \hfill Seattle, WA** \newline
*NCES Fellowship in Innovation Science for Education Analytics (ISEA) \hfill June 2025*

**Texas Association of School Business Officials (TASBO) \hfill Austin, TX** \newline
*Registered Texas School Business Administrator (RTSBA) \hfill September 2020*

**Vanderbilt University \hfill Nashville, TN** \newline
*Master's of Public Policy in Education Policy and Leadership — GPA 3.92 \hfill May 2014*

**University of Notre Dame \hfill Notre Dame, IN** \newline
*Bachelor of Arts, cum laude — GPA 3.78 \hfill May 2005*

## PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE 

**Houston Independent School District (HISD) \hfill August 2014 - September 2025**   \newline
*Director, Finance Project Management* \newline
*Deputy Director, Data Science & Analytics* \newline
*Grant Manager & Program Analyst, Research & Accountability*

**Strategic Data Leadership & Student Impact**

- Led cohort-based enrollment analysis and campus incentive planning, retaining 4,570 more students than projected by trendline, preserving $65.7M in state funding through 4-year mobility modeling and campus-level retention tools.
- Combined public, district, and change-of-address data to identify overlooked McKinney-Vento students, finding 498 additional eligible students, a 25% lift over the 2,000 baseline count, contributing about $1.9M in additional state funding.
- Designed *k*-means clustering models for 250+ schools, segmenting campuses into 6 groups for a state grant application and helping the CAO and CFO target interventions through principal component analysis.
- Directed HISD's pre-K analytics workstream, evaluating 165 campuses annually and informing 32 new classrooms at 26 schools, increasing from 10,500 to 12,000 seats through analyses of demand, facility, staffing, and policy constraints.
- Built and improved ArcGIS "Seats Available Today" models and business rules, supporting 160 schools and 200 daily users while reducing refresh time from 3 days to 30 minutes, enabling faster family enrollment and transportation decisions.
- Allocated $200M in proposed bond investments across pre-K priorities, planning 4,000 new seats to raise capacity from 12,000 to 16,000 and expand access to all 4-year-olds in the district eligible for free full-day pre-K.
- Determined pre-K expansion feasibility across 160 schools, identifying 20 viable campuses and 12 requiring major renovation, with each approved site adding 22 seats through floorplans, walkthroughs, Google Earth, and code reviews.
- Built a multivariate scoring model for pre-K expansion prioritization, ranking 160+ campuses and informing 32 classrooms at 26 schools, or 700 seats, replacing ad hoc principal requests with a districtwide strategy.
- Reframed district policy around pre-K supply and demand, creating a shared planning vocabulary used in dashboards, policy documents, and board communications, accelerating decisions on expansion and marketing priorities across functions.
- Modeled PK4-to-kindergarten attrition patterns, finding 34% attrition since SY18-19, with campuses ranging from under 10% to over 80% loss, informing retention incentives and digital and door-knocking outreach efforts.
- Designed continuous-improvement recommendations for bonus-driven human-capital programs, supporting 86% annual retention at 24 schools versus 68% district-wide across 250 schools by moving eligibility notifications before Spring Break.
- Supported Houston Education Research Consortium projects with cleaned datasets and technical expertise, enabling work across 4 research centers and informing 30 publications using 8 years of school-choice data and policy review.

**Causal Inference and Study Design**

- Managed district data operations for an IES-funded RAND randomized trial of Zearn Math, covering 32,314 students and 845 teachers across 64 schools in 3 years for study meeting WWC standards without reservation.
- Led HISD's transition from Woodcock-Johnson to CogAT for kindergarten GT screening, cutting testing time from 1,500 staff hours to 300 while setting local cut scores for 1,630 students through sampling strategy and Monte Carlo simulation.
- Served as PI for a randomized school-choice marketing study, testing 1 mailer across 115,000 enrolled students and documenting 2,000 returned pieces, showing leadership that 1-time mail outreach was insufficient.
- Authored 4 program evaluation reports, analyzing 250,495 student records and 12,098 teachers to identify significant STAAR gains and 82% retention of high-performing STEM teachers at grant-funded schools.
- Designed a causal-inference partnership with Education Analytics on performance-pay strategies, tracking 5,004 early-career STEM teachers across 24 schools over 5 years and showing retention bonuses were used 10 times more often.
- Authored methodology standards for district curriculum-provider evaluations, governing 6 providers with common learning outcomes and quasi-experimental design requirements, enabling leadership to compare products by cost and value.
- Co-wrote new HISD research-request policies and approval protocols, streamlining 60 annual studies by cutting review time from over 6 weeks to 3 and adopting 3 standard templates to govern data sharing and strengthen privacy protections.

**Data Analysis, Modeling, and Dissemination**

- Queried, cleaned, and analyzed 70+ complex datasets covering 275 schools for 9 years for grant program evaluations, over 2.1M student records, 117,750 teacher records, and 240,000 choice applications, informing program decisions.
- Designed and administered a compensation survey, collecting 2,482 responses from 10,790 teachers and weighting results across 195 subpopulations, surfacing that 54% said no dollar amount could offset an unsupportive principal. 
- Built student-teacher linkage and rostering for Panorama surveys, linking 122,000 students and 7,800 teachers across 250 campuses at 99.8% accuracy while replacing manual rostering and saving 1,200 hours.
- Built reproducible ETL pipelines for staffing decisions across 200 campuses, supporting twice-daily refreshes that informed 232 teacher reassignments and 210 positions eliminated, reducing overstaffing costs and improving ratio compliance. 
- Overhauled the Campus Demographic Report model and visualization, expanding access from 300 printed users to more than 20,000 online page views and improving Year 2 publication timing by 2 months. 
- Managed 10-year demographic forecasting with Population and Survey Analytics, modeling enrollment scenarios used for bond planning and 12 campus closure decisions, helping avoid investment in schools later identified for closure. 
- Modeled capital project cost estimates for 4 mutually exclusive operational plans, identifying a $2M range between lowest- and highest-cost options and showing the highest-risk scenario would cost most while preserving no additional value. 
- Drove process improvements for payroll and accounts-payable timeliness, reducing late hourly payroll errors from 3%, or 9,400 annually, to 1%, or 3,100 annually, improving employee trust while lowering correction costs. 
- Built an error-resolution workflow for student schedules and readiness-screening compliance, reducing 2,100 schedule errors across 160 schools to under 0.2% within 10 days with 99.8% compliance.

**Team Empowerment and Stakeholder Management**

- Led 7 analysts in building reproducible daily data products for executive decision-making, refreshing 6 daily products and cutting cycle time from 6 hours to under 1 hour, supporting decisions affecting 3,000 students.
- Conducted code reviews, QA testing, and coaching for 6 junior and 3 peer analysts, accelerating adoption of reusable modular scripts, reducing rework cycles, and improving turnaround and cross-project handoffs.
- Negotiated and revised external data-use agreements, completing 16 agreements/quarter while reducing review cycles from 3 months to 6 weeks, protecting FERPA compliance across 275 schools and 210,000 students annually.
- Managed annual Council of Great City Schools data submissions across district operations and academics, delivering 1,100 unique metrics without revised submissions, enabling benchmarking against 55 peer districts and quarterly internal KPIs.
- Served on the district Data Sharing Committee reviewing agreement language biweekly, revising 20 agreements per quarter over 3 years, standardizing templates and strengthening FERPA, COPPA, and consortium-aligned compliance.
- Led 3 major SchoolFinder releases, supporting families of 25,000 applicants in 2023-24 and 20,000 in 2024-25 while reducing refresh cycles from 3 work-weeks to under 3 hours.

**Program Evaluation, Grants, and Regulatory Compliance**

- Managed budgeting, evaluation, and implementation for 3 USDE discretionary grants, overseeing $62.3M across 7 years with on-time reporting, a successful 2019 site visit, and a FY16 Deloitte single audit with 0 findings.
- Closed federal grant spending on time and in scope across 7 years, closing the $14,998,280 84.374B grant with less than $10 unspent, or 99.9999% utilized, avoiding clawbacks and supporting a 1-year no-cost extension.
- Reconciled monthly grant actuals against federal and governmental accounting rules, reviewing every PO, quote, and check across 7 cost centers plus up to $100,000 in monthly payroll, restoring all 3 grants to schedule.
- Authored 20 semiannual reports to USDE, submitting all on time with satisfactory determinations for amendments and continued funding while tracking teacher retention, bonus uptake, STAAR gains, and minority-group-isolation outcomes.
- Negotiated scopes of work and monitored vendor/partner agreements, managing 70 contracts worth $37.4M over 7 years, protecting intellectual property, privacy, and service continuity while supporting STEM experiences for 16,500 students.
- Audited current contracts and data-sharing agreements, reviewing 150 agreements and finding over 75% missing critical FERPA clauses and about 80% lacking security constraints, triggering a full redesign of research-approval controls.
- Authored district legal templates for external data-access agreements, creating 2 full contract templates and 8 exhibits adopted in 100 agreements, reducing legal back-and-forth and restoring district control over deletion and privacy terms.
- Composed 8 standard National Student Data Privacy Agreement exhibits for procurement, affecting 160 contracts annually starting in 2024, reducing review time and negotiation while strengthening breach, retention, and access protections.
- Ensured compliance across 200+ elementary schools by flagging user errors and issuing daily corrective guidance, reducing 2,100 student-schedule errors to under 0.2% within 10 days with 99.8% compliance.
- Served on TEA's statewide Information Task Force shaping data policy and reporting schedules, helping drive changes adopted by all 1,016 districts and 20 service centers affecting 5.5M students statewide.

**Financial Controls and Budget Management**

- Led 12 cross-functional projects for the CFO and senior operations leaders over 9 months, supporting initiatives affecting $203.4M in payroll and a $30M supply operation across 110 departments.
- Overhauled the 500+ page Finance Manual for campus-based and departmental users, supporting 400 cost centers and training clerks from 350 of them, resolving prior audit findings while reducing purchase delays and escalations.
- Improved payroll timeliness through manager accountability and monitoring, reducing late hourly payroll errors from 3%, or 9,400 annually, to 1%, or 3,100 annually, lowering correction costs and improving employee trust.
- Allocated $200M in proposed bond investments using pre-K supply and demand analysis, planning 4,000 additional seats that would expand capacity from 12,000 to 16,000 and improve projected access for eligible 4-year-olds districtwide.
- Managed annual budgeting and project implementation for $62.3M in federal and matching grant expenditures, maintaining on-time reporting for 7 years and supporting 0 audit findings in FY16 through recurring monitoring and budget controls.
- Reconciled monthly grant actuals to EDGAR, GAAP, GASB, and FASRG rules, reviewing 7 cost centers and up to $120,000 in monthly payroll while restoring spending within 18 months and limiting forfeiture to 0.65% over 7+ years.
- Kept grant spending aligned and on schedule through closeout, fully utilizing the $14,998,280 TIF4 grant with less than $10 unspent, avoiding clawbacks and securing a no-cost extension while coordinating teams and vendors.

**Research Operations and Technical Infrastructure**

- Advised TEA takeover transition planning across HR, finance, planning, and accountability, helping reallocate 73% of the FY24 budget, or $1.6B, while revising budgets for 130 campuses and compensation for 16,000 employees.
- Managed data for a district audit of college, career, and military readiness programs, compiling 1,024,924 annual student records across 40 high schools and 6 readiness programs over 10 years.
- Integrated district, partner, and Census data to evaluate the Houston Food Bank collaboration, analyzing services reaching 120,000 students at 200 schools and over 1M meals across 3 programs, helping reduce duplication and improve coverage.
- Managed a 7-figure NASA Johnson Space Center partnership, overseeing an estimated $2.4M over 3 years serving 5 schools, 100 teachers, and 28,000 annual coaching and planning hours with 0 budget variance.

## PREVIOUS EXPERIENCE 

**Metro Nashville Public Schools \hfill Nashville, TN**  
*Summer Associate for Research and Policy \hfill May 2014 - July 2014*

**Promise Neighborhood at Martha O'Bryan Center \hfill Nashville, TN**  
*Practicum Intern and Professional Volunteer \hfill January 2013 - May 2014*

**Vanderbilt University \hfill Nashville, TN**  
*Graduate Research Assistant and Summer Program Evaluation Associate \hfill January 2012 - May 2014*

**Boys Hope Girls Hope of Colorado \hfill Denver, CO**  
*Development Director \hfill August 2010 - May 2011*

**Community Resource Center \hfill Denver, CO**  
*Program Associate and Project Coordinator \hfill June 2008 - August 2010*

**Robert B. Sharp Company \hfill Denver, CO**  
*Project Coordinator, Feasibility Studies & Capital Campaigns \hfill April 2007 - May 2008*

**El Pomar Foundation \hfill Colorado Springs, CO**  
*Program Coordinator and Associate Grants Officer \hfill July 2005 - April 2007*

## INVITATIONS AND AWARDS 

**Guest Speaker, Univ. of Illinois School of Information Sciences	\hfill November 2025**
*IS594 Change Catalyst*	

**Guest Speaker, Texas A\&M College of Education	\hfill October 2024**  
*EDAD 688 Education Policy Series*	

**Guest Speaker, Univ. of Illinois \hfill April 2023**  
*IS595 Competitive Intelligence & Knowledge Management*	

**Diversity Scholarship, RStudio now Posit PBC \hfill	July 2022**  
*posit::conf Conference and Workshop*	

**Featured Publication: TIF Program Evaluations \hfill October 2021**  
*ARNIEdocs Research, Evaluation, and Assessment in the Schools*

**Conference Presentation, Council of Great City Schools	\hfill October 2021**  
*“Making Performance Pay Pay Off”*	

**Guest Speaker, Rice University	\hfill November 2018**  
*SOCI 437/537 Sociology of Education*	

**Conference Presentation, CRSEA 9th Annual Conference	\hfill May 2015**  
*“Parents, Teachers, and Title I Schools”*	

## SELECT PUBLICATIONS

Pane, J., Doss, C., Todd, I., & Seaman, D. (2025, June). **Efficacy of Zearn Math over two years in grades 3 to 5: An experiment in Texas.** (EdWorkingPaper: 25-1211). Retrieved from Annenberg Institute at Brown University.

**Price, L.**, & Islam, F. (2025, February). **Technical Documentation: Local Norms & GT Matrix Scores for Students Entering Kindergarten in SY25-26**. HISD: Internal Publication.

**Price, L**., Lasode, M., & Powers, K. (2023, December). **Memo To Chief Financial Officer: FY25 Forecasting Models for Student Average Daily Attendance.** HISD: Internal Memo.

Lasode, M., **Price, L.**, Abdoli, M., & Hovey, I. (2022, July). **2021-2022 Campus Demographic Report.** HISD: Research and Accountability.

**Price, L**., & Scuello, M. (2022, May). **Evidence of Promise from the Magnet Schools Assistance Program: Recruiting Students to HISD’s Secondary STEM Magnet Schools.** HISD: Internal Publication.

**Price, L.**, & Matney, A. (2021, December). **Memo to Executive Officer, Student Support: Additional Homeless Students Identified as Eligible for Services Under McKinney-Vento**. HISD: Internal Memo.

<!-- Aulicino, C., Rinehart, C., & **Price, L.** (2021, October). **Secondary STEM Magnet Programs Study: Year 2\.** Metis Associates: Internal Publication for HISD. -->

<!-- Aulicino, C., Rinehart, C., & **Price, L.** (2020, September). **STEM Magnet Program Study: Year 1\.** Metis Associates: Internal Publication for HISD. -->

**Price, L.**, Christian, M., & Stevens, C. (2018a). ***A Matched-Comparison Analysis of Math and Science STAAR Scores: Teacher Incentive Fund STEM Grant in Houston ISD**.* HISD: Research and Accountability. ERIC Number ED603680.

**Price, L.**, Cramer, E., Milanowski, A., Mader, J., & Stevens, C. (2018b). ***A Human Capital Approach to Improving STEM Education: Teacher Incentive Fund STEM Grant in Houston ISD.*** HISD: Research and Accountability. ERIC Number ED603681.

**Price, L.**, Provencher, S., & Stevens, C. (2018c). ***A Descriptive Overview: Teacher Incentive Fund STEM Grant in Houston ISD***. HISD: Research and Accountability. ERIC Number ED603678.

<!-- **Price, L.**, & Stevens, C. (2017). ***Teacher Incentive Fund, Cohort 3—Looking Back, Around, And Ahead: HISD’s Viewfinder For Teacher And Leader Effectiveness***. HISD: Research and Accountability. -->

**Price, L.**, Jordan, S., & Stevens, C. (2016, January). ***Preferences and Perspectives on Teacher Compensation, 2014-2015***. HISD: Internal Publication.

**Price, L.** (2014). ***Parents and Teachers in Title I Schools: A Critical Race Theory Analysis of Parent Engagement*** \[Unpublished master’s thesis\]. Peabody College of Education and Human Development, Vanderbilt University.

## TECHNICAL SKILLS

**Programming and Data Tools:** R, Stata, Python, SQL, RStudio, R Markdown, Quarto, LaTeX, Git, GitHub, ETL, Relational Databases, Database Administration, Monte Carlo Simulation Modeling, *k*-means Clustering, Multivariate Modeling, Machine Learning, Statistical Analysis, Econometrics, Large-Scale Data Analysis, Data Modeling, Metadata

**Business Intelligence:** Power BI, Tableau, ArcGIS, Google Earth, PowerQuery, Microsoft Excel, Spreadsheets, Reporting Requirements, Presentations

**Enterprise and Administrative Systems:** PowerSchool SIS, SharePoint Administration, PeopleSoft, SAP ERP, Qualtrics, Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, Adobe Forms

**Research, Evaluation and Analytics Methodologies:** Randomized Controlled Trials, Causal Inference, Cohort Analysis, Continuous Improvement, Forecast Modeling, Program Evaluation, Program Assessment, Primary Research, Logic Models, Data Science, Data Analytics, Data Interpretation, Data Manipulation, Data Strategies, Data Systems

**Financial, Regulatory and Compliance Frameworks:** Federal Grants Management, EDGAR, GAAP, GASB, FASRG, FERPA, COPPA, CIPA, Privacy Regulations, Legal Requirements, Procurement Regulations, Data Privacy, Data Retention, National Student Data Privacy Agreement

**Data Governance and Operations:** Data Governance, Data Integrity, Data Management, Quality Assurance, Internal Controls Design, Research Approval Protocols, Compilation

**Leadership and Professional Competencies:** Leadership, Team Management, Project Management, Policy Analysis, Policy Writing, Business Process, Business Insights, Client Relations, Community Impact, Problem Solving, Written Communication
